task_completion.description: Verify the agent has opened the interface to create a new conversation in FreeScout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'New Conversation' modal or page is visible within the FreeScout web interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the customer email 'clarkeashley@example.com' is entered in the recipient field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'clarkeashley@example.com' is visible in the 'Customer', 'To', or 'Email' input field of the new conversation form.

task_completion.description: Verify the subject 'Peripheral compatibility' is entered in the subject line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Peripheral compatibility' is visible in the 'Subject' input field.

task_completion.description: Verify the body of the conversation mentions a peripheral compatibility issue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main text area for the message body contains text describing or mentioning a peripheral compatibility issue.

task_completion.description: Verify the conversation was successfully created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification (e.g., 'Conversation created') is visible, or the newly created conversation with the subject 'Peripheral compatibility' and customer 'clarkeashley@example.com' is displayed in the active conversation view or mailbox list.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeScout web interface rather than using terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeScout web UI in a web browser, not a terminal, command prompt, or code editor.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual FreeScout application and did not just type the required text into a text editor or dummy page.
integrity.visual_evidence: The surrounding UI elements (navigation menus, sidebars, mailbox folders) match the standard FreeScout application interface.

integrity.description: The agent created the ticket through the standard user interface step-by-step rather than directly manipulating the backend.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots shows the progressive filling of the new conversation form before submission.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected customer email is exactly 'clarkeashley@example.com'. The expected subject is exactly 'Peripheral compatibility'. These values originate from Ticket ID 2 of the Kaggle Customer Support Ticket Dataset.
